question_title: Will another U.S. cabinet member leave office before May 2026?
summary: Trump has fired 2 cabinet members in <30 days, and multiple credible outlets report he's actively considering firing 3+ more (Lutnick, Chavez-DeRemer, Gabbard) in the "next several weeks." The resolution window is ~22 days. While Trump often considers but doesn't act, the combination of multiple named targets with specific grievances, political urgency from midterms, and a demonstrated high propensity to fire makes YES the more likely outcome. Monte Carlo simulation gives ~62.5%; adjusting upward for the strength of multi-source reporting on active consideration yields ~75%.

factors.description: Two cabinet members fired in <30 days (Noem March, Bondi April 2), showing elevated firing rate and willingness to act
Multiple credible outlets (Politico, WaPo, NBC, CNN, Atlantic) with White House sources report Trump actively considering more firings - naming Lutnick, Chavez-DeRemer, and Gabbard specifically
Sources say changes expected 'in next several weeks' - timeline aligns with resolution window
Political motivation: midterm elections in November, low approval ratings, desire to show action while GOP controls Senate for confirmations
Inspector General investigation into Chavez-DeRemer expected to complete 'in coming weeks' - could force action
Trump himself downplayed rumors in phone interview with The Hill, said don't read into shake-up talk
Trump often considers firing officials but changes his mind before acting - CNN notes he 'wavers for months'
White House officially says all officials have 'full confidence' - though same was said about Bondi before firing
Senate confirmation constraints could delay action (need replacements lined up)
Active conflict with Iran may either distract Trump or accelerate personnel changes

reasoning:
My forecast: **0.75 (75%)** probability that another U.S. cabinet member will leave office before May 2026.

The key drivers are the demonstrated high firing rate (2 in <30 days), at least 3 named cabinet-level targets under active consideration with specific documented grievances, and sources explicitly stating changes are expected in "the next several weeks." The main counterweights are Trump's tendency to consider but not act, and his own public denial of further shake-ups — though similar denials preceded the Bondi firing.

resolution_criteria: This question will as Yes if, after April 5, 2026, and before May 1, 2026, a member of the Cabinet of the United States leaves office or is announced to leave office.
fine_print:
This question will consider the whole cabinet, including the designated cabinet-rank positions. If a position is dropped from cabinet-rank status, it will still count for purpose of this question.

Specifically this question will consider the following positions:

* President
* Vice President
* Secretary of State
* Secretary of the Treasury
* Secretary of Defense
* Attorney General
* Secretary of the Interior
* Secretary of Agriculture
* Secretary of Commerce
* Secretary of Labor
* Secretary of Health and Human Services
* Secretary of Housing and Urban Development
* Secretary of Transportation
* Secretary of Energy
* Secretary of Education
* Secretary of Veterans Affairs
* Secretary of Homeland Security
* Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency
* Director of the Office of Management and Budget
* Director of National Intelligence
* Director of the Central Intelligence Agency
* United States Trade Representative
* Administrator of the Small Business Administration
* White House Chief of Staff

Acting members will not be considered as members for this question.

To qualify, an announcement that a member will leave office without immediate effect must include a set date or period, and that date or period must end before August 2026.

If an individual leaves the cabinet due to being nominated for another listed position, that will not resolve this question even if they later fail to reenter cabinet.

***
This question's information (resolution criteria, fine print, background info, etc) is synced with an [original identical question](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43099) which opened on 2026-04-08 19:22:00. This question will resolve based on the resolution criteria and fine print of the linked original question. However, if this question would resolve differently than the original question, then this question will be annulled. Additionally, if the original question's resolution could have been known before this question opened, then this question will be annulled.
